rulings.Case Title: LAWRENCE LIN VS. ZEN COMPOUND, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion To Set Aside Default Judgment , Ntc Of Motion To Vacate; Dismissal
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, June 19, 2012, Line 2, PLAINTIFF LAWRENCE LIN'sMotion To Set Aside Default Judgment, Motion To Vacate; Dismissal. Denied. The moving party did not provide courtesy copies of the moving papers as required by Local Rule 2.6B. Moreover, as best as the court can determine, the motion is a very belated request for reconsideration of Judge Mason's September 1, 2011 order, which should have been made a long time ago. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID YANG et al VS. FOLSOM/ FIRST LLC A LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 4TH AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, June 19, 2012, Line 5, DEFENDANT CRESCENT HEIGHTS, INC., HOWARD S WRIGHT CONSTRUCTION CO., A CORPORATION, FOLSOM FIRST LLC's DEMURRER TO 4TH AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained without leave to amend as to the eighth and ninth causes of action. As conceded by Plaintiffs, the eighth cause of action was improperly included in the Fourth Amended Complaint. The ninth cause of action is barred by the statute of limitations. The Court rejects Plaintiffs' Errata, which attempts to amend this cause of action without leave of court. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID YANG et al VS. FOLSOM/ FIRST LLC A LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 4TH AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, June 19, 2012, Line 5, DEFENDANT THE METROPOLITAN ASSOCIATION, A CORPORATION's DEMURRER TO 4TH AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained without leave to amend as to the eighth cause of action and overruled as to all other claims. As conceded by Plaintiffs, the eighth cause of action was improperly included in the Fourth Amended Complaint. Plaintiffs do not lack standing to assert the first, fifth, sixth, seventh, and ninth causes of action. Plaintiffs owned the property at the commencement of this suit and are entitled to seek damages incurred during their ownership. Regarding the first cause of action, although Plaintiffs did not file the certificate required by Civil Code 1369.560, dismissal is not warranted because Plaintiffs would be substantially prejudiced by the delay in seeking dismissal on this ground. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID YANG et al VS. FOLSOM/ FIRST LLC A LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 4TH AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, June 19, 2012, Line 5, DEFENDANT THE METROPOLITAN ASSOCIATION, A CORPORATION's MOTION TO STRIKE 4TH AMENDED COMPLAINT. Denied as to attorney's fees and granted without leave to amend as to punitive damages. Plaintiffs do not lack standing to enforce the CC&Rs, so the claim for attorney's fees is proper. As conceded by Plaintiffs, the request for punitive damages was improperly included in the Fourth Amended Complaint. Defendant's request for sanctions is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CORZAC, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al VS. THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A MUNICIPAL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Further Discovery And Site Inspection
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 19, 2012, Line 3. Plaintiff's motion to compel further discovery and site inspection. Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is off calendar. On July 10, 2012, the Court entered an order staying this action pending the appeal of the ruling on the anti-SLAPP motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to katherinehuibonhoa@paulhastings.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: DELORIS COLLIER VS. VPSF, INC., DBA REAL FOOD COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Petition And Petition Of Deft To Compel Arbitration And Stay Court Proceedings And To Deny Entry Of Default Request
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, June 19, 2012, Line 13, DEFENDANTs VPSF, INC., DBA REAL FOOD COMPANY, TOM SERAFICA's Petition To Compel Arbitration And Stay Court Proceedings And To Deny Entry Of Default Request. Granted. This action is not excluded from the scope of the arbitration clause. Assuming that the discovery clause is an unconscionable provision, that clause is severed. The arbitration agreement allows for the recovery of FEHA attorney's fees. The FRCP clause is not an unconscionable provision. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: REGAL STONE LIMITED et al VS. LONGS DRUGS STORE CALIFRONIA, LLC A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To File First Amended Complaint Under Seal
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, June 19, 2012, Line 16, PLAINTIFFs REGAL STONE LIMITED, FLEET MANAGEMENT LTD's Motion To File First Amended Complaint Under Seal. Granted. No opposition filed and the motion complies with CRC 2.550. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the court will sign the proposed order submitted by the moving party without need for any appearances. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MELYSSA JO KELLY VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion Pursuant To Cal. Govt. Code 946.6 For Judicial Relkief From Bar To Sue Because Of Nonrepresentation Of Claim
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, June 19, 2012, Line 17, PETITIONER MELYSSA KELLY's Motion Pursuant To California Government Code section 946.6 For Judicial Relief From Bar To Sue Because Of Nonrepresentation Of Claim. Granted. Plaintiff has sufficiently met the elements for the relief she seeks. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: SUSAN PATRICIA GIBBS VS. MARTHA MOHR DAVIS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Martha Mohr Davis' Special Motion To Strike (Anti-Slapp) Request For Permanent Restrains Set To Be Heard May-30-12
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, June 19, 2012, Line 18, DEFENDANT MARTHA MOHR DAVIS' SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE (ANTI-SLAPP) and REQUEST FOR PERMANENT RESTRAINtS. Denied. Respondent failed to show the action arises out of protected activity. Reasonable attorney fees are awarded to Petitioner as this motion is wholly devoid of merit. None of the activities Petitioner seeks to enjoin implicate Respondent's free speech or petition rights. Respondent's concern that the restraining order will hinder communication between Respondent and her daughter are baseless as Respondent's daughter has not lived at Petitioner's house since February 4, 2012. Furthermore, the current policy governing phone calls between Respondent and her daughter does not provide for Respondent to initiate phone calls to her daughter and explicitly forbids Respondent from calling Petitioner's landline or cell phone. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)
